observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare adjusting entries of the ₱ 1.5 million LGU counterpart in the book of accounts of the LGU substantiated with complete documentation and furnish the Office of the Auditor with the same for verification.
The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documentary requirements (referring to ₱ 1.5 million) such as copy of the approved POW, authorization from Local Sanggunian and certification from the Municipal Accountant that funds previously transferred to Implementing Agency (IA) has been liquidated or justification why another fund transfer was made even previous fund transfers were unliquidated, in order for the Audit Team to continue with the post audit of the said transaction.
The Municipal Accountant to issue a written demand letter addressed to the District Engineer (DE) of Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) - Bukidnon Second District Engineering Office for the immediate submission of the copies of Fund Utilization Reports, Journal of Checks Issued (only related to the disbursements of the LGU counterparts) duly received by COA-DPWH and certified copies of the Journal Entry Voucher from DPWH taking up the disbursements of ₱ 4.25 million LGU counterparts. Once obtained, she must immediately prepare adjusting entries relative to the ₱ 4.25 million and submit the certified copies of the journal entry voucher, subsidiary ledger and other pertinent documents to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.
Hereafter, the Municipal Mayor to stop granting LGU counterpart to government agencies with unliquidated receivables.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatements in the financial statements as of December 31, 2024 duly substantiated with working papers and all other relevant documents and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification; Henceforth, she must disclose the said project in the Notes to Financial Statements.
The Municipal Accountant must immediately reconcile the difference noted of the total cost of the housing units amounted to ₱ 164,814.64 and submit its working papers and the like to COA for evaluation.
Hereafter, the Municipal Accountant to account and record the collections of the Kadingilan Paglaum Village Project in the Trust Fund; Transfer the ₱ 128,600 to the trust fund. The Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) of the transfer must be duly substantiated with complete supporting documents such as but not limited to:
Certified copy from the original of the subsidiary ledgers from the year the collections for Kadingilan Paglaum Village Project started up to the end of the year 2024; and
Other necessary documents that can help verify and validate the total amount to be transferred.
The Office of Auditor must be furnished of the photocopies of the JEV and all the supporting documents for compliance.
The Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit documents, reports records or the like showing the approved sales price of the housing units awarded to the actual occupants of the village and a list of delinquent occupants with their outstanding liabilities as of December 31, 2024 to COA for evaluation.
The Municipal Treasurer to immediately send demand letters to delinquent occupants of the Kadingilan Paglaum Village Project and furnish COA of the received copies thereof.
The Municipal Mayor to create a special inventory team to conduct site inspections and physical inventory of the residential houses at the Kadingilan Paglaum Village with the corresponding names of the actual occupants of the 35 units. The inventory report thereof must be submitted to the Municipal Housing and Resettlement Committee as their starting point to comply all the audit recommendations regarding the Paglaum Housing Projects.
The Municipal Mayor to request the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to update and create amendments of Resolution No. 2013-082 and fill-in the vacant positions of the Municipal Housing and Resettlement Committee.
If proven that there are no existing contracts, the Municipal Mayor or the Chairman of the Municipal Housing and Resettlement Committee must immediately enter into a contract of agreement with the actual occupants of the Kadingilan Paglaum Village citing, among others, the payment schemes of the occupants like monthly amortization and possible sanctions like penalties and eviction in case of non-payment; Furnish COA with the certified copies of the same for reference.
Aside from the Municipal Housing and Resettlement Committee, the Municipal Mayor must immediately designate a focal person to oversee the management of the Kadingilan Paglaum Village Project.
All documents, reports, records and the like gatherer relative to the Kadingilan Paglaum Village Project shall form as permanent files of the Municipal Housing and Resettlement Committee and provide copies to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare adjusting entries to derecognize the costs of the demolished structures and their corresponding accumulated depreciation from the books of accounts of the LGU.
Further, the Municipal Accountant must prepare the depreciation schedule of these properties as of December 31, 2024 and submit it together with other pertinent documents to COA for evaluation.
The Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the inventory reports of the scrap materials from the six (6) demolished structures to the audit team for review and verification.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to do the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in resolving the glitches pertaining to the Approved Program of Work (APOW) of the project and immediately submit liquidation report to the Provincial Accounting Office once these deficiencies have been resolved.
The Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the completed project from “CIP-Infrastructure Assets” to “Road Networks” amounted to ₱ 14,995,675.24 in the Trust Fund book duly substantiated with complete documentary supports and furnish the audit team with the same for review and verification. Further, follow-up the Deed of Donation from the Provincial Government of Bukidnon and, once obtained, transfer the asset immediately to the General Fund duly supported with complete documentation. Furnish the audit team with the certified copies of the same for evaluation.
Hereafter, the Municipal Accountant to immediately reclassify completed projects, if to be capitalized, to its appropriate asset once it is already 100% complete.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
The Municipal Engineer to immediately submit an application for relief of accountability of the impaired service vehicle duly substantiated with the seven (7) documentary requirements abovementioned including a valid and satisfactory explanation for the incurrence of delay in submitting them to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification.
The Municipal Engineer to immediately submit a copy of an approved vehicle trip ticket, Travel Order of the passenger/s and Certificate of Appearance on the day (April 27, Saturday) the road crash happened to the Office of the Auditor for verification.
The Municipal Engineer to directly submit additional evidences, aside from the traffic incident report, to the Office of the Auditor that will prove that the driver of the service vehicle (Toyota Hilux) was not at fault or negligent and exercised due diligence and care while driving such as but not limited to:
Survey of the crash scene;
Witness Statements;
Photographs from the accident scene;
Accident reconstruction drawing/sketch; and
Relevant Medical Records
The Municipal Engineer together with the GSO-Designate must follow-up straightaway the release of the check for the insurance claim of the damaged service vehicle amounted to ₱ 578,054.44 from GSIS in writing and furnish a copy of the said follow-up letter to the Office of the Auditor; Start the restoration of the damaged service vehicle once the claim is received.
Henceforth, the GSO-Designate must use the Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR), new form, instead of the Acknowledgment Receipt for the Equipment (ARE), old form, in dealing with issuance of equipment to its respective end-user and must indicate there-in the date the property is issued by the GSO-Designate and the date the property is received by the end-user.

We recommended to the LSB the following courses of action:
For the PSDS as co-chairpersons of the LSB to:
Formally clarify the submission process with the DepEd CO to have a clear understanding of where and how the quarterly SEF utilization reports should be submitted.
Ensure the regular submission of the reports to the DepEd CO once the process is clarified.
For the LSB Chairperson to direct the LSB Secretary to:
Provide the Local Treasurer his copy of the SEF budget strictly within the indicated timeframe.
Furnish the local sanggunian and DBM regional office their copy of the quarterly SEF utilization report to adhere with the requirements of Section 6.1 of DepEd DBM DILG JC 2017-01.
Obtain and submit to COA a circular, guideline or letter from the DILG confirming that posting the SEF utilization reports on the FDPP is sufficient in lieu of physically furnishing a copy to the DILG regional office. If such a circular, guideline or letter is not available, the LSB must comply with the requirement to physically furnish the report.
Ensure that the submission of reports and furnishing of its copies to the intended recipients, are properly supported with stamped transmittal letters confirming receipt.

We recommended to the management to do following courses of action:
Submit a written explanation and supporting documents justifying the disbursement of ₱1,046,245.00, including the basis for the continued funding of COVID-19 related PPAs from the 20% LDF despite the expiration of relevant legal issuances.
The written explanation/justification must include the detailed utilization report of the medicine and medical supplies worth ₱ 770,345.00 and the Fuel Consumption Reports including the Trip Tickets for the fuel expenses worth ₱ 275,900. Submit also the file copies of all the documents related to the “on-going threat of the pandemic” COVID-19 in the Municipality of Kadingilan in the year 2024.
Cease further utilization of the 20% LDF for COVID-19 related expenditures. The LGU should ensure compliance with the expiration of policies tied to the State of Calamity and Public Health Emergency. Any unspent appropriations should be realigned to priority infrastructure, social, and economic development programs in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1 (February 22, 2017) and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC 2020-1 (November 4, 2020).
Refrain from allocating the 20% LDF for COVID-19-related expenditures in subsequent budget cycles.
Conduct regular reviews of budget allocations and policy issuances to prevent similar irregularities in the future. Close coordination with the DILG and DBM is recommended to ensure adherence to updated guidelines.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit a written justification for why the Purchase Requests (PRs) prices per liter were significantly above the Province of Bukidnon's Prevailing Retail Pump Price issued by the Department of Energy website to the office of the auditor for review and validation.
Henceforth, we recommended to the management to consider the retail pump price issued by the DEO and procure its POL purchases through competitive bidding under Appendix 22 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, for the purpose of economies of scale.

We recommended to the Human Resource Management Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and immediately submit the lacking documentary requirements as cited in Annex I to the Office of the Auditor to continue with the post audit of the disbursements.
Henceforth, payments of terminal leave benefits must be completely and properly supported with the required documentary supports abovementioned.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
The Municipal Agriculturist, with the Municipal Mayor’s consent, shall lease/rent the Municipal Food Terminal (including its equipment, furniture and fixtures) out to private entities/individuals at a very reasonable rate after its goods for sale last. This is the best solution to easily address the noted deficiencies. This is also less hassle on the part of the management. Nonetheless, let it be clearly stipulated in the contract of lease that the lessee abides with the conditions of the Department of Agriculture Region 10, particularly on its intended purpose, which helped in the establishment of the Municipal Food Terminal.
Further, he must instruct the storekeeper to stop purchasing goods for sale as soon as the management is fully decided to lease out the Municipal Food Terminal, to account all the funds of her safe-keeping after December 31, 2024 until goods for sale last or the time the Municipal Food Terminal will stop selling  and prepare the required financial reports verified and certified by the Municipal Accountant and to remit all the monies of the Municipal Food Terminal for the said period to the Municipal Treasurer. Furthermore, he must pull-out the storekeeper and assign her with new responsibilities once the Municipal Food Terminal is already leased out. Additionally, he must furnish the audit team with all the pertinent reports, documents and the like for verification.
The Municipal Accountant to review and verify the financial reports for CY 2024 of the Municipal Food Terminal as provided by the storekeeper and establish the correct balances of the following:
Total Account Receivables as of December 31, 2024
Total Sales (cash and credit) for CY 2024
Total Purchases for CY 2024
Total Expenses for CY 2024
Total Net Income for CY 2024
Total Inventory as of December 31, 2024
Total Assets as of December 31, 2024
Total Liabilities as of December 31, 2024
Total Government Equity as of December 31, 2024
She must prepare adjusting entries substantiated with working papers and the like if necessary to ensure that everything about the Municipal Food Terminal is properly accounted and recorded in the books of accounts of LGU-Kadingilan. Submit certified copies of the same to the audit team for verification.
The Municipal Agriculturist must instruct the storekeeper to make a cut off of credit, to prepare a schedule of accounts receivable of the Municipal Food Terminal as of cut-off date and to stop granting credit to LGU personnel. He must seek assistance with the Municipal Accountant to verify the schedule of accounts receivable prepared by the storekeeper. Once validated and certified by the Municipal Accountant, he must instruct the storekeeper to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to fully collect all the accounts receivables as of cut-off date on or before the 1st quarter of CY 2025 ends. Demand letters must be issued and copies must be furnished to the audit team for reference.
If the Municipal Food Terminal (including its equipment, furniture and fixtures) will be leased/rented out, the Municipal Mayor must coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an ordinance for its monthly lease or rental rate to assure that the income generated there-in is proper, valid and legal. Furnish COA of the ordinance for reference as and compliance.

We recommended to the management to submit a letter from the DILG Regional Director approving the foreign travel of the selected officials and employees to the Republic of South Korea to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, being no approval thereof it would be subjected to refund the whole amount of ₱ 2,465,640.00.
If the travel is confirmed approved and authorized, submit a revised travel itinerary that includes the computation of Daily Subsistence Allowance as outlined in Section 14 of Executive Order No. 77. Any excess claims should be refunded to the LGU’s coffers.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action.
The Municipal Mayor to:
Submit to the audit team the lacking supporting documents enumerated in the Roman Numeral No. I and II, including a travel itinerary that includes the computation of DSA as outlined in Section 14 of EO 77, for further review.
Direct the Municipal Vice Mayor to submit to the audit team the lacking supporting documents for his foreign travel enumerated in the Roman Numeral No. I and II, including a revised travel itinerary that includes the computation of DSA as outlined in Section 14 of EO 77. Also, provide a written explanation or justification for the differences noted in the attached versions of Itinerary. All for further review of the audit team.
The management to stop, if not, minimize foreign travels and evaluate future foreign travels with thorough cost-benefit analysis and consider alternative options that minimize government spending while achieving the same objectives. Strictly observe prudence and judiciousness in the utilization of public funds.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
To immediately submit to the audit team the noted lacking documents to substantiate the disbursements and for the audit team to continue with the post-audit of the transactions.
For the Municipal Treasurer to:
Submit copies of the valid IDs of the representatives who claimed the HEA on behalf of the original intended recipients, and
Submit individual certifications from (1) recipients whose HEAs were claimed by a representative, and (2) recipients with unsigned payrolls. The certification must state that the HEA was duly received and explicitly indicate the amount, purpose and period of receipt.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:

The Municipal Assessor must immediately furnish the Municipal Treasurer with the assessment roll as of December 2022.

The Municipal Treasurer must immediately update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card by considering the assessment roll provided by the Municipal Assessor. He then prepares the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for CY 2023 and furnishes the Municipal Accountant. From there, the Municipal Accountant must prepare adjusting entries if there are understatements in the RPT and SET Receivables already established and recorded in CY 2023 and furnish the Office of the Auditor with the certified copies of the journal entry vouchers, subsidiary ledgers and other pertinent documents.

Henceforth, the Municipal Assessor must provide the Municipal Treasurer with the assessment roll on or before December 31 of every year as a great tool to fortify the controls in checking the correctness of the information entered into the computer system.

Hereafter, the Municipal Treasurer must prepare and provide the Municipal Accountant with the duly certified lists showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for establishing and recording the current RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: Non-submission of the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to do the following courses of action:

To immediately prepare the adjusting entries relative to the ₱ 2.95 million unexpended DRRMF in CY 2016 and 2017 which was erroneously debited to the account “Due from Other Funds” in the General Fund book of accounts and submit certified copies of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), subsidiary ledgers Page 4 of 4 AOM No. 2024-005(2023): Kadingilan, Bukidnon of the affected accounts and other pertinent documentary supports to the Office of the Auditor; and

Henceforth, to strictly adhere to Annex C of similar COA Circular 2012-002 for the correct accounting entries in transferring unspent DRRMF from General Fund to Trust Fund book of accounts.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:

The Municipal Mayor to require the regular conduct of physical count of the agency’s “Biological Assets”, that is at least once a year to determine and verify its existence and submit to the office of the Auditor the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Biological Assets for review and verification in accordance with Section 124 of National Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs).

The Municipal Accountant and MGSO-Designate to prepare and maintain the required ledger cards and stock cards after determination of the assets’ existence in accordance with Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

The Municipal Accountant must coordinate with the MGSO-Designate and Municipal Agriculture Officer to reconcile the books of accounts with the results of physical inventory and submit the working papers and/or reconciling statements relative therefrom to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant must always adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 27 in relation to the measurement, recognition of gains or losses reporting and disclosure of the “Biological Assets” account.
observations.reason: Only few records related to the account were recovered, there are more to look for and into.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to do the following courses of action:

To immediately reclassify the ending balance of “Other Deferred Credits” account in the Special Education Fund book of accounts amounted to ₱ 312,691.21 which also refers to the unearned “Special Education Tax” in CY 2023 to “Deferred Special Education Fund Tax” account and submit the certified copies of the journal entry voucher, subsidiary ledgers and other pertinent documents to the Office of the Auditor; and

Henceforth, all advance payments of SET shall be booked or credited to “Deferred SEF Tax” account. When earned, she must immediately recognize Page 4 of 4 AOM No. 2024-009(2023): Kadingilan, Bukidnon income particularly “Special Education Tax” in the Special Education Fund book of accounts.

We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts including the accumulated depreciation in the books of the LGU. Moreover, review the prior year projects related to road rehabilitation/maintenance recorded as assets and adjust the same duly supported with working papers showing all the details/information particularly the actual scope of works to prove that only maintenance works were undertaken on the projects.

Henceforth, we further recommended to the management that all future projects relative to rehabilitation/maintenance of road projects that qualify as regular maintenance shall be expensed outright.

We recommended to the management to immediately submit a written justification to the Office of the Auditor, for evaluation, why the disclosure pertaining to the one (1) unit pick-up truck (NAVARA) in the Notes to Financial Statements didn’t confirm to the results of our audit.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:

The Municipal Health Officer must coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in securing the updated or revised MOA, guidelines or the like from the management of Southern Bukidnon Inter-Local Health Zone (SBILHZ) relative to LGU Kadingilan’s cost sharing that will further substantiate the payment of ₱ Page 3 of 3 AOM No. 2024-08(2023): Kadingilan, Bukidnon 50,000.00; Submit it immediately to the Office of the Auditor to continue with the post audit of transaction.

Hereafter, the Municipal Treasurer must stop paying the Southern Bukidnon Inter-Local Health Zone (SBILHZ), through the Municipal Treasurer of Maramag, Bukidnon, for the LGU’s cost sharing if the updated or revised MOA, guidelines or the like cannot be secured by the Municipal Health Officer.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of the unexpended LGU counterparts of the fully completed projects in the amount of ₱ 494,561.11 from the Trust Fund to the General Fund.

We further recommended to the management that the said amount must be reverted to unappropriated surplus or as one of the sources of funds for a supplemental appropriation of the LGU.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to review the transactions recorded in the accounts “Due From Special Accounts”, “Due to Special Accounts” and “Government Equity” accounts. Moreover, prepare adjusting entries to properly present the correct amount of the accounts duly supported with working papers to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

Henceforth, we further recommended the recording of the receipt of share from the Internal Revenue Collection and the transfer of 20% share of IRC to the Local Development Fund Special Account following the aforementioned table.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:

The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant must coordinate in regularly setting the obligation for electricity expenses of the various Municipal facilities and waterworks operation incurred in December every end of the year. Page 4 of 4 AOM No. 2024-001 (2023): Kadingilan, Bukidnon Projecting total amount close to the total actual monthly expense is advised to establish the payable or amount to be obligated if Statements of Accounts (SOAs) are not readily available yet; and

The Municipal Budget Officer must stop from charging previously incurred expenses such as electricity expenses to current appropriations or “Current Operating Expenditures”.

We recommended to the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman (BAC) the following courses of action:

To immediately submit a legal basis, if there are any, that allows procurements above the threshold of ₱ 200,000.00 for a third-class municipality, to use alternative mode of procurement such as negotiated procurement particularly small value procurement;

Otherwise, submit a written justification why the procurement of sand and gravel which were purchased on the same date, from the same supplier and more than the threshold were not done thru public bidding to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation; and

To strictly adhere to the 2016 Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184 in all procurements of LGU Kadingilan.

We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer the following courses of action:

To immediately prepare and maintain duly notarized contracts of lease between all the lessees of the cemetery and the Municipal Mayor immediately; Page 4 of 4 AOM No. 2024-002 (2023): Kadingilan, Bukidnon

To maintain and prepare individual ledger cards of all the lessees straightaway;

To directly submit monitoring reports showing the maturity terms of the leases of all lessees per leased section, breakdown of the ₱ 358,625.37 and all other pertinent financial reports related thereto to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification; and

To fully take on the responsibility of supervising, controlling and managing the cemetery operations of the LGU.
observations.reason: Contract of lease was not yet prepared by the MTO for its pro-forma was not immediately secured.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:

The Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO must immediately reconcile the variance amounting to ₱ 184,391.00 and submit the reconciling statements, working papers or the like to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification.

After reconciling, the Municipal Accountant must prepare the necessary adjusting entries to return the over-transferred amount in the TF to GF.

Hereafter, the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant must submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day of each month and prepare the report strictly in accordance with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Henceforth. the Municipal Accountant must always disclose the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund including the unexpended balance for the current and previous years in the NTFS.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:

The MPDC must immediately submit the revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) as approved by the local sanggunian and Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon recommendation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) in accordance with the approved LDRRM Plan to support the release and utilization in CY 2023 of the unexpended LDRRMF for years 2021 and 2022.

Henceforth, the MPDC and MDRRMO must ensure that future charges against the unexpended LDRRMF shall be sufficiently supported with (a) LDRRMF Investment Plan including under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged against the unexpended LDRRMF, and (b) revised AIP as approved by the local sanggunian and LCE upon Page 4 of 12 AOM No. 2024-013(2023): Kadingilan, Bukidnon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP.

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO must immediately reconcile the variance amounting to ₱ 34,952.80 and submit the reconciling statements, working papers or the like to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification.

We recommended to the management to submit a clear and definite legal basis that superseded the Notice of Eligibility (NOE) issued by the DILG Secretary and authorized the LGU to use the 58% rate in computing and granting the FY 2022 Performance-Based Bonus, otherwise, refund the excess payment of P225,898.64.
observations.reason: Management had already submitted a communication reply from OIC of DILG in relation to the grant of the FY 2022 PBB using the 58% rate, however, this is still subject for review, verification and evaluation by the audit team and for further audit decisions if warranted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry immediately, duly and completely supported with pertinent documentary requirements, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor to recognize and record the agricultural equipment from DA Regional Field Office 10 with a total amount of ₱ 1.589 million based on the suggested journal entry below:

Dr: Agricultural & Forest Equip
Cr: Government Equity
Further, we recommended to the Municipal Agriculturist to directly furnish the Municipal Accountant of similar Certificates of Turn-over that he will be receiving in the near future for the latter’s reference in preparing the required Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to account the receipt of the assets.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant went to DA- Region X asking for Property Transfer Receipt (PTR) as basis for Recording but the DA- Region X were not able to give the said documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to hasten the reconciliation of the unidentified “beginning balances” subsidiary ledgers of the mentioned real accounts to establish fair presentation, reliability, and accuracy of the Statement of Financial Position in the succeeding year.

Further, we recommended to the management to conduct one-time cleansing for Property, Plant and Equipment accounts required in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

Furthermore, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant to request for write-off of uncollectible receivables to COA.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is on-going; One-time cleansing is a work in progress
observations.reason: Tracing of the unidentified balances is a challenge; Searching for the pertinent records is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to implement COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with a reliable PPE balances to be presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management is already taking small steps to realize the said audit recommendation.
observations.reason: One time cleansing of “Motor Vehicles” account and “Construction and Heavy Equipment” account are on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Effect a correcting journal entry for items recorded as “Account Receivable” that should have been recorded to the appropriate “Due from” accounts;
Transmit a copy to the Office of the Auditor the Journal Entry Vouchers of the correction of the erroneous entries together with the working papers and supporting documents; and
Recognize “Accounts Receivable” only for receivables arising from the regular trade and business transactions of the LGU, and the appropriate “Due from” accounts for other receivables, as defined in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
1. The Municipal Assessor must immediately furnish the Municipal Treasurer with the assessment roll as of December 2022.
2. The Municipal Treasurer must immediately update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card by considering the assessment roll provided by the Municipal Assessor. He then prepares the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for CY 2023 and furnishes the Municipal Accountant. From there, the Municipal Accountant must prepare adjusting entries if there are understatements in the RPT and SET Receivables already established and recorded in CY 2023 and furnish the Office of the Auditor with the certified copies of the journal entry vouchers, subsidiary ledgers and other pertinent documents.
3. Henceforth, the Municipal Assessor must provide the Municipal Treasurer with the assessment roll on or before December 31 of every year as a great tool to fortify the controls in checking the correctness of the information entered into the computer system.
4. Hereafter, the Municipal Treasurer must prepare and provide the Municipal Accountant with the duly certified lists showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for establishing and recording the current RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to do the following courses of action:
1. To immediately prepare the adjusting entries relative to the ₱ 2.95 million unexpended DRRMF in CY 2016 and 2017 which was erroneously debited to the account “Due from Other Funds” in the General Fund book of accounts and submit certified copies of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), subsidiary ledgers Page 4 of 4 AOM No. 2024-005(2023): Kadingilan, Bukidnon of the affected accounts and other pertinent documentary supports to the Office of the Auditor; and
2. Henceforth, to strictly adhere to Annex C of similar COA Circular 2012-002 for the correct accounting entries in transferring unspent DRRMF from General Fund to Trust Fund book of accounts.

We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
1. The Municipal Mayor to require the regular conduct of physical count of the agency’s “Biological Assets”, that is at least once a year to determine and verify its existence and submit to the office of the Auditor the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Biological Assets for review and verification in accordance with Section 124 of National Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs).
2. The Municipal Accountant and MGSO-Designate to prepare and maintain the required ledger cards and stock cards after determination of the assets’ existence in accordance with Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
3. The Municipal Accountant must coordinate with the MGSO-Designate and Municipal Agriculture Officer to reconcile the books of accounts with the results of physical inventory and submit the working papers and/or reconciling statements relative therefrom to the Office of the Auditor for verification.
4. Lastly, the Municipal Accountant must always adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 27 in relation to the measurement, recognition of gains or losses reporting and disclosure of the “Biological Assets” account.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to do the following courses of action:
1. To immediately reclassify the ending balance of “Other Deferred Credits” account in the Special Education Fund book of accounts amounted to ₱ 312,691.21 which also refers to the unearned “Special Education Tax” in CY 2023 to “Deferred Special Education Fund Tax” account and submit the certified copies of the journal entry voucher, subsidiary ledgers and other pertinent documents to the Office of the Auditor; and
2. Henceforth, all advance payments of SET shall be booked or credited to “Deferred SEF Tax” account. When earned, she must immediately recognize Page 4 of 4 AOM No. 2024-009(2023): Kadingilan, Bukidnon income particularly “Special Education Tax” in the Special Education Fund book of accounts.

We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts including the accumulated depreciation in the books of the LGU. Moreover, review the prior year projects related to road rehabilitation/maintenance recorded as assets and adjust the same duly supported with working papers showing all the details/information particularly the actual scope of works to prove that only maintenance works were undertaken on the projects.
Henceforth, we further recommended to the management that all future projects relative to rehabilitation/maintenance of road projects that qualify as regular maintenance shall be expensed outright.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to institute measures to be able to compile on time the Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls and Official Receipts including its supporting documents. We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to debit the ‘Construction Materials Inventory’ account upon receipt/payment of the construction materials; and credit the same upon issuance of the materials for use in the construction with a corresponding debit entry to the account ‘Construction in Progress’.
We recommended to the management to direct the Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the misclassification and post the same in the appropriate subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Effect a correcting journal entry for items recorded as “Account Receivable” that should have been recorded to the appropriate “Due from” accounts;
Transmit a copy to the Office of the Auditor the Journal Entry Vouchers of the correction of the erroneous entries together with the working papers and supporting documents; and
Recognize “Accounts Receivable” only for receivables arising from the regular trade and business transactions of the LGU, and the appropriate “Due from” accounts for other receivables, as defined in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the year-end financial statements be submitted on or before February 14 of the ensuing year, as required in Section 41 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 347 of R.A. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2010-01.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare subsidiary ledgers for the three bank accounts under the general fund in order to hasten the reconciliation of balances among cashbooks, accounting ledgers and bank statements.

We recommended that effective immediately all cash advances of the Disbursing Officers must be supported with all the necessary documents enumerated in COA Circular No. 97-002 in order to validate the accuracy of the amount of the cash advance and minimize excess cash advances.
